title: thymus chamaedrys subsp. montanus
intro: Thymus chamaedrys subsp. montanus, also known as the mountain thyme or Alpine thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, perennial herb native to mountainous regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it effective against various infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of mountain thyme include its potential to boost the immune system, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, and improve digestion. Mountain thyme can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in culinary dishes, where its piney flavor and aroma add a unique character.
